insight:
Yesterday was a solid final prep day! Your 3.6km run at 142 bpm average shows you kept things appropriately easy before race day, and 9.7 hours of sleep was excellent recovery preparation. With 13,113 steps and 2,821 calories burned, your body is primed and ready.

Your HRV at 112ms sits perfectly in your baseline range, and that 46 bpm resting heart rate shows your cardiovascular system is well-adapted and recovered. The low stress reading of 30/100 confirms you're in an ideal state for tomorrow's half marathon - no need to modify anything today.

Since you're burning significantly more calories than your 2,300 target (521 over yesterday), focus on adequate carb loading today without overthinking it. Aim for familiar, easily digestible foods and maintain that protein target of 175g to support your muscles through tomorrow's 13.1 miles. You're ready!
